title: Getting started with NMFprofileR
text: NMFprofileR runs full multi-rank consensus Non-negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) transcriptomics matrix layers g:Profiler functional enrichment top. rank k fits consensus NMF, assigns genes samples factors, runs enrichment, computes silhouette widths, (optionally) writes tree tables figures disk. heavy steps depend NMF package live g:Profiler network calls, chunks shown executed. lightweight steps run real.

title: Getting started with NMFprofileR
text: NMFprofileR() orchestrates whole workflow. writes results output_prefix returns nmf_profile object everything memory. result keeps fitted objects, gene assignments, enrichment tables never re-read files: Pass write_files = FALSE compute entirely memory without creating files тАУ useful scripting, examples, tests.
code: result <- NMFprofileR(   expression_data = example_expression_data,   nmf_rank        = 2:5,   output_prefix   = file.path(tempdir(), "NMF_demo"),   nmf_nrun        = 20,   gprofiler_sources = c("GO:BP", "GO:MF", "GO:CC", "REAC", "TF") ) result #> <nmf_profile> #>   Ranks fitted : 2, 3, 4, 5 #>   Samples      : 53 #>   Runtime      : 3.2 mins #>   g:Profiler   : e111_eg58_p18_... #>   Output dir   : .../NMF_demo_Results summary(result)              # consolidated per-factor metrics across ranks result$fits[["3"]]           # fitted NMF object for k = 3 result$basis_genes[["3"]]    # every gene -> its dominant factor (argmax) result$marker_genes[["3"]]   # factor-specific marker genes (specificity-scored) result$sample_assignments[["3"]] result$enrichment$combined[["3"]]

title: Getting started with NMFprofileR
text: first stage validates filters matrix. requires gene-symbol rownames, resolves duplicated symbols (on_duplicate_genes, default "collapse_max"), drops -zero genes, selects highly-variable genes. variance_scale = "log" ranks variability log2(x + 1) instead raw scale, reduces mean-variance confound count data. matrix fed NMF always original non-negative scale.
code: m <- nmf_preprocess(   example_expression_data,   expression_threshold = 10,   variance_quantile    = 0.9 ) #> ! Dropping 794 all-zero gene row(s). #> тД╣ Filtering genes by mean expression < 10... #> тД╣ Filtering genes by variance < 0.9 quantile (raw scale)... dim(m) #> [1] 1574   53

title: Getting started with NMFprofileR
text: Every stage exported works memory, can build custom workflow inspect intermediate results.
code: fit     <- nmf_fit(m, rank = 3, nrun = 20) genes   <- nmf_basis_genes(fit)          # argmax assignment (all genes) markers <- nmf_marker_genes(fit)         # specificity-scored markers (subset) samples <- nmf_sample_assignments(fit)   # sample -> factor + silhouette  gene_sets <- split(genes$Gene, genes$Factor) enr <- nmf_enrichment(gene_sets, background_genes = rownames(m))

title: Getting started with NMFprofileR
text: nmf_rank_diagnostics() lays rank-selection metrics side side. real run pass result$rank_metrics; small illustrative table: common heuristic pick largest rank cophenetic correlation drops sharply, cross-checked silhouette peak.
code: rank_metrics <- data.frame(   rank       = 2:5,   cophenetic = c(0.98, 0.95, 0.90, 0.86),   dispersion = c(0.90, 0.82, 0.75, 0.70),   silhouette = c(0.61, 0.55, 0.48, 0.44) ) nmf_rank_diagnostics(rank_metrics) #> # A tibble: 12 ├Ч 3 #>     rank metric     value #>    <dbl> <chr>      <dbl> #>  1     2 cophenetic  0.98 #>  2     3 cophenetic  0.95 #>  3     4 cophenetic  0.9  #>  4     5 cophenetic  0.86 #>  5     2 dispersion  0.9  #>  6     3 dispersion  0.82 #>  7     4 dispersion  0.75 #>  8     5 dispersion  0.7  #>  9     2 silhouette  0.61 #> 10     3 silhouette  0.55 #> 11     4 silhouette  0.48 #> 12     5 silhouette  0.44

title: Getting started with NMFprofileR
text: Every run records manifest (parameters, package version, runtime), exact g:Profiler database version behind enrichment, full session information, UMAP embedding seeded тАУ result can traced inputs data snapshot produced (result$provenance).

title: Getting started with NMFprofileR
text: NMFprofileR depends Bioconductor packages ComplexHeatmap circlize. installing GitHub, enable Bioconductor repositories first:
code: setRepositories(ind = c(1, 2))   # CRAN + Bioconductor devtools::install_github("MilesGithub/NMFprofileR")

title: Batch analysis across many cohorts
text: common workflow run NMF profiling many independent expression matrices тАУ different cohorts, subgroups, tissues, conditions тАУ compare factors found across . run_nmf_batch() drives NMFprofileR() named list cohorts returns single consolidated summary tagged cohort, results can pooled traced back source. driver : resilient тАУ one cohort failing abort batch (on_error = "continue"); resumable тАУ cohorts whose results already exist skipped loaded disk (skip_existing = TRUE); self-describing тАУ every summary row carries cohortтАЩs Run_ID.
code: library(NMFprofileR) data("example_expression_data") dim(example_expression_data) #> [1] 20501    53

title: Batch analysis across many cohorts
text: cohort just expression matrix form NMFprofileR() accepts (genes rows gene-symbol rownames, samples columns). cohorts named list; names become run_id output subdirectory cohort. split bundled example data two illustrative cohorts: practice cohorts might come splitting one matrix grouping variable, reading several matrices disk named list.
code: half <- ncol(example_expression_data) %/% 2 cohorts <- list(   cohort_A = example_expression_data[, seq_len(half)],   cohort_B = example_expression_data[, (half + 1):ncol(example_expression_data)] ) vapply(cohorts, ncol, integer(1)) #> cohort_A cohort_B  #>       26       27

title: Batch analysis across many cohorts
text: Every argument ones driver manages (expression_data, output_prefix, run_id) passed straight NMFprofileR(). cohortтАЩs outputs land output_dir/<name>_Results/, consolidated summary also written output_dir/Batch_Consolidated_Summary.tsv. consolidated table per-factor columns single run, leading Run_ID:
code: batch <- run_nmf_batch(   cohorts,   output_dir        = file.path(tempdir(), "batch_demo"),   nmf_rank          = 2:4,   nmf_nrun          = 20,   gprofiler_sources = c("GO:BP", "REAC"),   enrichment_cache  = file.path(tempdir(), "gprofiler_cache") )  batch$consolidated   # one row per factor, per rank, per cohort (tagged Run_ID) batch$skipped        # cohorts skipped because results already existed batch$failures       # cohorts that errored (Cohort, Reason) #>     Run_ID Rank   Factor Num_Samples Percent_Samples #> 1 cohort_A    2 Factor_1          14            53.8 #> 2 cohort_A    2 Factor_2          12            46.2 #> 3 cohort_B    2 Factor_1          15            57.7

title: Batch analysis across many cohorts
text: skip_existing = TRUE default, re-running call interruption computes cohorts finished yet; completed ones read back saved nmf_profile bundle (Consolidated_Summary.tsv) still included batch$consolidated. Set skip_existing = FALSE force every cohort re-run.

what: Making a batch fast and robust
title: Batch analysis across many cohorts
text: Cache enrichment. Pass enrichment_cache = <dir> repeated g:Profiler queries (common cohorts share genes, re-running) served disk instead network. Queries keyed hash genes, background, sources, settings. Parallelize fits. Pass nmf_parallel = TRUE (optionally nmf_cores) spread cohortтАЩs consensus runs across local cluster. fixed nmf_seed parallel path returns factorization sequential one. Keep going failure. on_error = "continue" (default) cohort errors recorded batch$failures batch proceeds; use on_error = "stop" fail fast. Skip second enrichment pass. Marker-gene enrichment opt-(emit_marker_genes = FALSE default), halves number g:Profiler queries per cohort.

title: Batch analysis across many cohorts
text: heavy steps depend NMF package live g:Profiler network calls, batch chunk shown executed; cohort-building chunks run real.
code: utils::sessionInfo()

title: Consensus NMF Profiling and Functional Enrichment of Transcriptomic Data
text: NMFprofileR R package comprehensive multi-rank Non-negative Matrix Factorization (NMF) analysis transcriptomic data. provides automated rank estimation, factor-specific gene assignment, gene set enrichment analysis, publication-quality plots consolidated summary consensus maps.

title: Consensus NMF Profiling and Functional Enrichment of Transcriptomic Data
text: Multi-rank NMF reproducible results (brunet, lee, others). Composable, exported pipeline stages returning results memory, nmf_profile result object. Per-factor basis gene assignment (argmax) plus specificity-scored marker genes. Automated generation rank survey/diagnostics plots, consensus maps, factor correspondence heatmaps, consolidated summary metrics. Supports user-defined gene backgrounds enrichment analysis. Reproducibility: run manifest, g:Profiler database version, session info.

title: Consensus NMF Profiling and Functional Enrichment of Transcriptomic Data
text: Install directly GitHub using devtools. NMFprofileR depends Bioconductor packages ComplexHeatmap circlize, make Bioconductor repositories available first (otherwise install fails resolve ):
code: # install.packages(c("devtools", "BiocManager")) setRepositories(ind = c(1, 2))   # enable CRAN + Bioconductor # or: options(repos = BiocManager::repositories())  devtools::install_github("MilesGithub/NMFprofileR")

title: Consensus NMF Profiling and Functional Enrichment of Transcriptomic Data
text: NMFprofileR() returns nmf_profile object carries results memory (addition writing disk): Set write_files = FALSE compute entirely memory without creating files. pipeline stages also exported composable use: nmf_preprocess(), nmf_fit(), nmf_basis_genes(), nmf_marker_genes(), nmf_sample_assignments(), nmf_enrichment(), nmf_rank_diagnostics().
code: library(NMFprofileR)  # Load example expression data (genes x samples) data("example_expression_data")  # Run the NMFprofileR pipeline result <- NMFprofileR(   expression_data          = example_expression_data,   nmf_rank                 = 2:5,   output_prefix            = "results/NMF",   nmf_method               = "brunet",   nmf_nrun                 = 20,   gprofiler_organism       = "hsapiens",   gprofiler_sources        = c("GO:BP","GO:MF","GO:CC","REAC","TF"),   gprofiler_cutoff         = 0.05 ) result                       # compact overview (print method) summary(result)              # consolidated per-factor metrics across ranks result$fits[["3"]]           # fitted NMF object for k = 3 result$basis_genes[["3"]]    # gene -> dominant factor table result$marker_genes[["3"]]   # factor-specific marker genes

title: Consensus NMF Profiling and Functional Enrichment of Transcriptomic Data
text: output_prefix = "results/NMF" pipeline writes results/NMF_Results/: NMF_Core_Objects/ тАФ fitted NMF objects per rank (.rds). Basis_Genes/ тАФ per-factor gene assignments marker genes. Enrichment_Results/ тАФ per-factor combined enrichment tables. Sample_Assignments/ тАФ sample--factor assignments silhouette widths. Plots/ тАФ heatmaps, consensus/diagnostic maps, UMAP, enrichment plots. Summaries/ тАФ consolidated summary, run manifest, session info.

title: Consensus NMF Profiling and Functional Enrichment of Transcriptomic Data
text: Rank Survey Plot тАУ metrics across tested ranks. Consensus Map тАУ reproducibility factorization. Factor Correspondence Heatmap тАУ identifies similar factors across ranks. Consolidated Summary Heatmap тАУ combined factor-level metrics ranks.

title: Consensus NMF Profiling and Functional Enrichment of Transcriptomic Data
text: Also cite underlying methods: Gaujoux, R., & Seoighe, C. (2010). flexible R package nonnegative matrix factorization. BMC Bioinformatics, 11, 367. Raudvere, U., Kolberg, L., Kuzmin, ., Arak, T., Adler, P., Peterson, H., & Vilo, J. (2019). g:Profiler: web server functional enrichment analysis conversions gene lists. Nucleic Acids Research, 47(W1), W191тАУW198.
code: Miles Mee, 2025. NMFprofileR: Comprehensive multi-rank NMF analysis and enrichment in R. GitHub repository: https://github.com/MilesGithub/NMFprofileR

what: Perform a Full Multi-Rank NMF and Functional Enrichment Analysis тАФ NMFprofileR
title: Perform a Full Multi-Rank NMF and Functional Enrichment Analysis тАФ NMFprofileR
text: function main entry point NMFprofileR workflow. orchestrates entire analysis pipeline, preprocessing input expression matrix performing multi-rank NMF analysis, extracting basis genes, running functional enrichment, generating comprehensive suite visualizations summary reports.

title: Perform a Full Multi-Rank NMF and Functional Enrichment Analysis тАФ NMFprofileR
code: NMFprofileR(   expression_data,   nmf_rank = 2:5,   output_prefix,   gene_list_filter_file = NULL,   expression_threshold = 10,   variance_quantile = 0.7,   nmf_method = "brunet",   nmf_nrun = 20,   nmf_seed = 123456,   gprofiler_organism = "hsapiens",   gprofiler_sources = c("GO:BP", "GO:CC", "GO:MF", "REAC", "TF"),   gprofiler_correction = "g_SCS",   gprofiler_cutoff = 0.05,   enrichment_plot_top_n = 50,   verbose = FALSE,   write_files = TRUE,   on_duplicate_genes = c("collapse_max", "collapse_mean", "error"),   emit_marker_genes = FALSE,   variance_scale = c("raw", "log"),   custom_theme = NULL,   factor_palette = NULL,   umap_n_neighbors = 15,   run_id = NULL,   nmf_parallel = FALSE,   nmf_cores = NULL,   max_query_size = 10000,   enrichment_cache = NULL,   basis_gene_method = c("argmax", "specificity"),   stability_nboot = 0,   stability_frac = 0.8 )

title: Perform a Full Multi-Rank NMF and Functional Enrichment Analysis тАФ NMFprofileR
text: expression_data data frame matrix normalized, non-negative gene expression values. Rows represent genes (gene symbols rownames) columns represent samples. nmf_rank integer vector specifying factorization ranks (k) test (e.g., `2:5`). output_prefix character string defining base path file name prefix output files directories. example, `"results/MyAnalysis"`. gene_list_filter_file optional character string path plain text file containing list gene symbols (one per line). provided, expression matrix pre-filtered include genes. expression_threshold numeric value. Genes mean expression across samples threshold removed prior NMF. variance_quantile numeric value 0 1. mean-based filtering, genes variance quantile removed. step enriches informative genes. nmf_method character string specifying NMF algorithm use. Defaults 'brunet'. See `NMF::nmf` documentation options. nmf_nrun integer specifying number runs consensus NMF. higher number (e.g., 50-100) provides stable results. nmf_seed integer use random seed reproducibility. gprofiler_organism character string specifying organism g:Profiler enrichment analysis (e.g., 'hsapiens', 'mmusculus'). gprofiler_sources character vector data sources query g:Profiler (e.g., c("GO:BP", "REAC", "KEGG")). gprofiler_correction character string specifying multiple-testing correction method used g:Profiler. Defaults `"g_SCS"`, g:Profiler's native Set Counts Sizes method, accounts overlapping, hierarchical structure GO pathway terms generally appropriate (less conservative) Bonferroni Benjamini- Hochberg FDR. query uses custom background genes survive preprocessing (`custom_bg`), enrichment assessed tested gene universe rather whole genome. Alternatives (`"fdr"`, `"bonferroni"`) accepted reviewer familiarity loss power. gprofiler_cutoff numeric value significance threshold enrichment results. enrichment_plot_top_n integer specifying number top enriched terms display summary dot plots. verbose logical value. TRUE, prints detailed messages. Defaults FALSE. write_files logical value. TRUE (default), results, plots, log files written disk `output_prefix`. FALSE, nothing written directories created; pipeline computes memory returns results . Useful programmatic use, examples, tests (must write outside `tempdir()`). on_duplicate_genes handle duplicated gene symbols rownames `expression_data`: `"collapse_max"` (default) keeps per-sample maximum across duplicate rows (expressed probe), `"collapse_mean"` averages , `"error"` stops message. emit_marker_genes logical value. TRUE, addition argmax basis-gene assignment pipeline also extracts factor-specific marker genes (`NMF::extractFeatures`, Kim-Park specificity) runs enrichment , emitting `Marker_Genes_*` `Marker_Enrichment_*` outputs alongside argmax results. Defaults FALSE: markers opt-computing runs second g:Profiler query per rank (roughly doubling network work), matters batch use. argmax assignment always primary output. Regardless setting return value always carries `marker_genes` `enrichment$markers` elements (empty FALSE). variance_scale Either `"raw"` (default) `"log"`. scale gene variance measured highly-variable-gene filter. `expression_data` expected normalized, non-negative values (e.g. normalized counts TPM); data `expression_threshold = 10` raw-scale variance meaningful. `"log"` ranks variability `log2(x + 1)`, reducing mean-variance confound count data. matrix fed NMF always original (raw, non-negative) scale regardless setting. custom_theme optional `ggplot2` theme object applied ggplot-based plots (factor-summary bar plot, enrichment dot plots, sample UMAP) place package's built-theme. `NULL` (default) uses built-theme. factor_palette optional character vector colours used colour NMF factors across plots. `NULL` (default) uses built-palette. rank factors supplied colours, palette extended `grDevices::hcl.colors()`. umap_n_neighbors Integer; number neighbours sample-coefficient UMAP embedding (default 15). UMAP drawn cohort samples value, effective neighbour count capped `n_samples - 1` smaller cohorts still embed. run_id optional identifier run. supplied stamped leading `Run_ID` column `consolidated_summary_df` (-disk `Consolidated_Summary.tsv`) recorded run manifest, results many runs can pooled traced back source. nmf_parallel logical value. `FALSE` (default) per-rank consensus runs fitted sequentially. `TRUE` spread across local cluster; see [nmf_fit()] details. fixed `nmf_seed` parallel path yields factorization sequential one. rank-estimation survey always runs sequentially. nmf_cores Number worker processes use `nmf_parallel = TRUE`. `NULL` (default) uses one fewer number detected cores. max_query_size integer. Factor gene sets larger skipped (warning) rather sent g:Profiler, guarding pathological oversized queries. Defaults 10000. enrichment_cache optional path directory used cache g:Profiler results. set, query keyed hash genes, background, sources, settings read / written directory, re-runs (e.g. across batch) skip network. `NULL` (default) disables caching. basis_gene_method genes assigned factors primary basis-gene output (exported `Basis_Genes_*` tables, enrichment input, heatmap grouping). `"argmax"` (default) assigns every gene dominant factor; `"specificity"` instead uses sharper Kim-Park specificity markers (`NMF::extractFeatures`), leaving many genes unassigned. Changing changes primary scientific output. stability_nboot Integer. greater 0, factor stability assessed rank refitting `stability_nboot` random subsamples samples (see [nmf_stability()]); `Stability` column added `consolidated_summary_df` per-rank tibbles returned `$stability`. Defaults 0 (), multiplies fitting cost. stability_frac Fraction samples drawn stability subsample (default 0.8); used `stability_nboot > 0`.

title: Perform a Full Multi-Rank NMF and Functional Enrichment Analysis тАФ NMFprofileR
text: Invisibly, S3 object class `nmf_profile` (list underneath,   `$` access works) `print()` `summary()` methods. Elements: `consolidated_summary_df` data frame summarizing every factor       every rank tested. `rank_metrics` data frame quality metrics       `NMF::nmfEstimateRank` rank survey (empty survey failed). `fits` named list fitted `NMFfit` objects, keyed rank. `basis_genes` named list per-rank basis-gene tables (every       gene dominant factor, argmax). `marker_genes` named list per-rank factor-specific marker-gene       tables (specificity-scored; `emit_marker_genes = TRUE`). `sample_assignments` named list per-rank sample-assignment       tables (sample dominant factor, silhouette widths). `enrichment` list `per_factor`, `combined`, `markers`       named lists per-rank g:Profiler enrichment data frames. `nmf_rds` named list file paths saved NMF fit objects       (empty `write_files = FALSE`). `output_dirs` named list output directories created       run, `NULL` `write_files = FALSE`. `failures` data frame (columns `Rank`, `Reason`) ranks whose       NMF fit failed skipped; empty every rank succeeded. `stability` named list (rank) per-factor stability tibbles,       present `stability_nboot > 0` (otherwise empty list). `runtime` `difftime` giving total pipeline runtime. `provenance` named list recording captured g:Profiler       database version (`gprofiler_version`), `run_parameters` used,       paths manifest session-information files written       (`files`), reproducibility. Unless `write_files = FALSE`, detailed results, plots, log files   additionally written disk location specified `output_prefix`.

title: Perform a Full Multi-Rank NMF and Functional Enrichment Analysis тАФ NMFprofileR
text: NMF run sequentially (across parallel backend), keeps results reproducible fixed `nmf_seed` avoids failure mode NMF's parallel execution called within package. fixed `nmf_seed` factorization therefore deterministic within fixed computational environment; exact numerical reproducibility across machines can still affected BLAS implementation threading. Every run also writes manifest session information (surfaced `provenance` element return value) result can traced parameters g:Profiler database snapshot produced .

what: Align NMF factors across runs by basis-loading correlation тАФ align_factors
title: Align NMF factors across runs by basis-loading correlation тАФ align_factors
text: Matches factors several NMF runs common reference "" factor can tracked across runs (e.g. across cohorts, ranks, resamples). first element `x` reference; every run's factors correlated reference factors shared genes matched one--one.

title: Align NMF factors across runs by basis-loading correlation тАФ align_factors
code: align_factors(   x,   method = c("greedy", "hungarian"),   min_cor = 0.5,   cor_method = c("pearson", "spearman") )

title: Align NMF factors across runs by basis-loading correlation тАФ align_factors
text: x named list (length >= 2) `NMFfit` objects /basis matrices (genes rows gene-symbol rownames, factors columns). names identify runs; first element reference. method pick one--one matches: `"greedy"` (default) repeatedly takes highest remaining correlation; `"hungarian"` finds globally optimal assignment via `clue::solve_LSAP()` (used `clue` package installed, otherwise falls back greedy). min_cor Minimum absolute correlation pair reported match (default 0.5). cor_method Correlation method, `"pearson"` (default) `"spearman"`.

title: Align NMF factors across runs by basis-loading correlation тАФ align_factors
text: list : `reference` name reference run. `correlations` tibble (`run`, `factor`, `ref_factor`,       `correlation`) every run factor every reference factor. `matches` tibble (`run`, `factor`, `ref_factor`, `correlation`)       selected one--one matches `abs(correlation) >= min_cor`.

title: Align NMF factors across runs by basis-loading correlation тАФ align_factors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit_a <- nmf_fit(m, rank = 3) fit_b <- nmf_fit(m, rank = 3, seed = 99) aln <- align_factors(list(run_a = fit_a, run_b = fit_b)) aln$matches } # }

title: Assign genes to their dominant NMF factor тАФ nmf_basis_genes
code: nmf_basis_genes(fit)

title: Assign genes to their dominant NMF factor тАФ nmf_basis_genes
text: fit `NMFfit` object [nmf_fit()].

title: Assign genes to their dominant NMF factor тАФ nmf_basis_genes
text: tibble columns `Gene` `Factor` (factor levels   `Factor_1 ... Factor_k`).

title: Assign genes to their dominant NMF factor тАФ nmf_basis_genes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ basis_genes <- nmf_basis_genes(fit) split(basis_genes$Gene, basis_genes$Factor) } # }

what: Run g:Profiler enrichment on NMF factor gene sets тАФ nmf_enrichment
title: Run g:Profiler enrichment on NMF factor gene sets тАФ nmf_enrichment
text: Runs per-factor combined g:Profiler functional enrichment list factor gene sets, memory (nothing written disk).

title: Run g:Profiler enrichment on NMF factor gene sets тАФ nmf_enrichment
code: nmf_enrichment(   basis_genes_list,   background_genes,   organism = "hsapiens",   sources = c("GO:BP", "GO:CC", "GO:MF", "REAC", "TF"),   correction = "g_SCS",   cutoff = 0.05,   max_query_size = 10000,   enrichment_cache = NULL )

title: Run g:Profiler enrichment on NMF factor gene sets тАФ nmf_enrichment
text: basis_genes_list named list character vectors, one gene set per factor (e.g. splitting [nmf_basis_genes()]). background_genes character vector background gene symbols. organism, sources, correction, cutoff g:Profiler query settings; see [NMFprofileR()] details. max_query_size Integer; gene sets larger skipped rather sent g:Profiler (default 10000). enrichment_cache Optional path directory used cache g:Profiler results query hash; `NULL` (default) disables caching. See [NMFprofileR()].

title: Run g:Profiler enrichment on NMF factor gene sets тАФ nmf_enrichment
text: list two elements: `per_factor` (list `gost` result   objects, one per factor, `NULL` results) `combined`   (data frame enrichment across union factor genes).

title: Run g:Profiler enrichment on NMF factor gene sets тАФ nmf_enrichment
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ bg <- rownames(m) gene_sets <- split(basis_genes$Gene, basis_genes$Factor) enr <- nmf_enrichment(gene_sets, background_genes = bg) } # }

what: Fit consensus NMF at a single rank тАФ nmf_fit
title: Fit consensus NMF at a single rank тАФ nmf_fit
text: thin, -memory wrapper around [NMF::nmf()] package's platform-aware parallel handling error trapping. Returns fit object without writing anything disk.

title: Fit consensus NMF at a single rank тАФ nmf_fit
code: nmf_fit(   expr_matrix,   rank,   method = "brunet",   nrun = 20,   seed = 123456,   nmf_parallel = FALSE,   n_cores = NULL )

title: Fit consensus NMF at a single rank тАФ nmf_fit
text: expr_matrix preprocessed, non-negative numeric matrix (see [nmf_preprocess()]). rank factorization rank (integer). method NMF algorithm (e.g. `"brunet"`). nrun number consensus runs. seed integer seed reproducibility. nmf_parallel Logical; `FALSE` (default) `nrun` consensus runs executed sequentially. `TRUE` spread across local PSOCK cluster. parallel path opt-NMF's default parallel backend fails NMF invoked inside another package (workers see NMF namespace); cluster configured load NMF worker, fixes , NMF pre-generates RNG stream every run, yields results identical sequential path fixed `seed`. Falls back sequential (warning) cluster created. n_cores Number worker processes use `nmf_parallel = TRUE`. `NULL` (default) uses one fewer number detected cores, capped `nrun`.

title: Fit consensus NMF at a single rank тАФ nmf_fit
text: `NMFfit` object, `NULL` fit fails.

title: Fit consensus NMF at a single rank тАФ nmf_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ m <- nmf_preprocess(example_expression_data, expression_threshold = 0,                     variance_quantile = 0) fit <- nmf_fit(m, rank = 3, nrun = 10) } # }

what: Extract factor-specific marker genes by specificity score тАФ nmf_marker_genes
title: Extract factor-specific marker genes by specificity score тАФ nmf_marker_genes
text: Unlike [nmf_basis_genes()] (assigns *every* gene dominant factor argmax), selects genes specifically associated factor, using `NMF::extractFeatures()` (Kim & Park specificity scoring). result smaller, sharper set marker genes per factor; many genes intentionally left unassigned.

title: Extract factor-specific marker genes by specificity score тАФ nmf_marker_genes
code: nmf_marker_genes(fit)

title: Extract factor-specific marker genes by specificity score тАФ nmf_marker_genes
text: fit `NMFfit` object [nmf_fit()].

title: Extract factor-specific marker genes by specificity score тАФ nmf_marker_genes
text: tibble columns `Gene` `Factor`. Factors specific   markers contribute rows.

title: Extract factor-specific marker genes by specificity score тАФ nmf_marker_genes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ markers <- nmf_marker_genes(fit) split(markers$Gene, markers$Factor) } # }

what: Preprocess an expression matrix for NMF тАФ nmf_preprocess
title: Preprocess an expression matrix for NMF тАФ nmf_preprocess
text: Validates input (numeric matrix, gene-symbol rownames, duplicate-symbol resolution, -zero row/column checks) applies standard filtering (optional gene-list filter, mean-expression threshold, variance quantile, non-negativity). first stage [NMFprofileR()] pipeline, exposed composable use.

title: Preprocess an expression matrix for NMF тАФ nmf_preprocess
code: nmf_preprocess(   expression_data,   expression_threshold = 10,   variance_quantile = 0.7,   gene_list_filter_file = NULL,   on_duplicate_genes = c("collapse_max", "collapse_mean", "error"),   variance_scale = c("raw", "log") )

title: Preprocess an expression matrix for NMF тАФ nmf_preprocess
text: expression_data data frame matrix normalized, non-negative gene expression values. Rows represent genes (gene symbols rownames) columns represent samples. expression_threshold numeric value. Genes mean expression across samples threshold removed prior NMF. variance_quantile numeric value 0 1. mean-based filtering, genes variance quantile removed. step enriches informative genes. gene_list_filter_file optional character string path plain text file containing list gene symbols (one per line). provided, expression matrix pre-filtered include genes. on_duplicate_genes handle duplicated gene symbols rownames `expression_data`: `"collapse_max"` (default) keeps per-sample maximum across duplicate rows (expressed probe), `"collapse_mean"` averages , `"error"` stops message. variance_scale Either `"raw"` (default) `"log"`. scale gene variance measured highly-variable-gene filter. `expression_data` expected normalized, non-negative values (e.g. normalized counts TPM); data `expression_threshold = 10` raw-scale variance meaningful. `"log"` ranks variability `log2(x + 1)`, reducing mean-variance confound count data. matrix fed NMF always original (raw, non-negative) scale regardless setting.

title: Preprocess an expression matrix for NMF тАФ nmf_preprocess
text: filtered, non-negative numeric matrix ready [nmf_fit()].

title: Preprocess an expression matrix for NMF тАФ nmf_preprocess
code: data("example_expression_data") m <- nmf_preprocess(example_expression_data, expression_threshold = 0,                     variance_quantile = 0) #> ! Dropping 794 all-zero gene row(s). #> тД╣ Filtering genes by mean expression < 0... #> тД╣ Filtering genes by variance < 0 quantile (raw scale)... dim(m) #> [1] 19707    53

what: Project new samples onto learned NMF factors тАФ nmf_project
title: Project new samples onto learned NMF factors тАФ nmf_project
text: Scores new samples existing factorization solving coefficient matrix H basis matrix W held fixed (`min ||V - W H||` s.t. `H >= 0`), using non-negative multiplicative updates. Genes aligned shared symbols W new data.

title: Project new samples onto learned NMF factors тАФ nmf_project
code: nmf_project(fit, newdata, n_iter = 200L, tol = 1e-06)

title: Project new samples onto learned NMF factors тАФ nmf_project
text: fit `NMFfit` object (numeric basis matrix W, genes x factors) defining learned factors. newdata numeric matrix data frame new samples (genes rows gene-symbol rownames, samples columns). n_iter Maximum number multiplicative-update iterations (default 200). tol Convergence tolerance maximum change H (default 1e-6).

title: Project new samples onto learned NMF factors тАФ nmf_project
text: data frame one row per new sample: `SampleID`, one coefficient   column per factor (`Factor_1 ... Factor_k`), `Dominant_Factor`.

title: Project new samples onto learned NMF factors тАФ nmf_project
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ fit <- nmf_fit(m, rank = 3) nmf_project(fit, newdata = m_new) } # }

what: Tidy NMF rank-selection diagnostics тАФ nmf_rank_diagnostics
title: Tidy NMF rank-selection diagnostics тАФ nmf_rank_diagnostics
text: Lays key rank-selection metrics side side across tested ranks "authoritative" component count can chosen (typically largest cophenetic correlation drops, silhouette peak). Works `rank_metrics` produced [NMFprofileR()] (via `NMF::nmfEstimateRank`).

title: Tidy NMF rank-selection diagnostics тАФ nmf_rank_diagnostics
code: nmf_rank_diagnostics(x, metrics = c("cophenetic", "dispersion", "silhouette"))

title: Tidy NMF rank-selection diagnostics тАФ nmf_rank_diagnostics
text: x `nmf_profile` object [NMFprofileR()], `rank_metrics` data frame directly. metrics Character vector metrics extract; matched case-insensitively prefix (`"silhouette"` matches `silhouette.consensus`). Metrics present data silently skipped.

title: Tidy NMF rank-selection diagnostics тАФ nmf_rank_diagnostics
text: tibble long form columns `rank`, `metric`, `value`.

title: Tidy NMF rank-selection diagnostics тАФ nmf_rank_diagnostics
code: rm <- data.frame(rank = 2:4, cophenetic = c(0.99, 0.95, 0.88),                  dispersion = c(0.9, 0.8, 0.7), silhouette = c(0.6, 0.5, 0.4)) nmf_rank_diagnostics(rm) #> # A tibble: 9 ├Ч 3 #>    rank metric     value #>   <dbl> <chr>      <dbl> #> 1     2 cophenetic  0.99 #> 2     3 cophenetic  0.95 #> 3     4 cophenetic  0.88 #> 4     2 dispersion  0.9  #> 5     3 dispersion  0.8  #> 6     4 dispersion  0.7  #> 7     2 silhouette  0.6  #> 8     3 silhouette  0.5  #> 9     4 silhouette  0.4

what: Assign samples to their dominant NMF factor with silhouette widths тАФ nmf_sample_assignments
title: Assign samples to their dominant NMF factor with silhouette widths тАФ nmf_sample_assignments
text: Assigns every sample dominant coefficient factor (argmax coefficient matrix) attaches consensus-matrix silhouette width sample.

title: Assign samples to their dominant NMF factor with silhouette widths тАФ nmf_sample_assignments
code: nmf_sample_assignments(fit, verbose = FALSE)

title: Assign samples to their dominant NMF factor with silhouette widths тАФ nmf_sample_assignments
text: fit `NMFfit` object [nmf_fit()]. verbose Logical; passed silhouette computation diagnostic messages.

title: Assign samples to their dominant NMF factor with silhouette widths тАФ nmf_sample_assignments
text: data frame columns `SampleID`, `Dominant_Factor`,   `Silhouette_NMF`, ordered factor descending silhouette.

title: Assign samples to their dominant NMF factor with silhouette widths тАФ nmf_sample_assignments
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nmf_sample_assignments(fit) } # }

what: Assess NMF factor stability by subsampling тАФ nmf_stability
title: Assess NMF factor stability by subsampling тАФ nmf_stability
text: Quantifies reproducible factor refitting NMF random subsets samples measuring well resampled factors recover reference factors (fitted full matrix). resample basis loadings correlated reference reference factor's best match recorded; per-factor stability mean best matches across resamples (1 = perfectly reproducible).

title: Assess NMF factor stability by subsampling тАФ nmf_stability
code: nmf_stability(   expr_matrix,   rank,   method = "brunet",   nrun = 10,   seed = 123456,   nboot = 30,   subsample_frac = 0.8,   nmf_parallel = FALSE,   n_cores = NULL )

title: Assess NMF factor stability by subsampling тАФ nmf_stability
text: expr_matrix preprocessed, non-negative numeric matrix (genes x samples), [nmf_preprocess()]. rank factorization rank (integer). method, nrun, seed Passed [nmf_fit()] every fit. nboot Number subsampling iterations (default 30). subsample_frac Fraction samples drawn (without replacement) iteration (default 0.8). nmf_parallel, n_cores Passed [nmf_fit()].

title: Assess NMF factor stability by subsampling тАФ nmf_stability
text: tibble columns `Factor` `Stability` (mean best-match   correlation across resamples), one row per reference factor.

title: Assess NMF factor stability by subsampling тАФ nmf_stability
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ m <- nmf_preprocess(example_expression_data, expression_threshold = 10,                     variance_quantile = 0.9) nmf_stability(m, rank = 3, nrun = 10, nboot = 20) } # }

what: Run NMFprofileR over many cohorts and consolidate the results тАФ run_nmf_batch
title: Run NMFprofileR over many cohorts and consolidate the results тАФ run_nmf_batch
text: batch driver applies [NMFprofileR()] several expression matrices ("cohorts"), writing cohort's outputs subdirectory `output_dir` returning single consolidated per-factor summary across cohorts. resilient (one cohort failing abort batch), resumable (already-completed cohorts can skipped), tags every row cohort's `run_id` results can pooled traced.

title: Run NMFprofileR over many cohorts and consolidate the results тАФ run_nmf_batch
code: run_nmf_batch(   cohorts,   output_dir,   ...,   skip_existing = TRUE,   on_error = c("continue", "stop"),   write_batch_summary = TRUE )

title: Run NMFprofileR over many cohorts and consolidate the results тАФ run_nmf_batch
text: cohorts named list expression matrices data frames (one per cohort), form accepted [NMFprofileR()]. names used `run_id` output subdirectory cohort. output_dir directory cohort's `<name>_Results` tree written. ... arguments passed [NMFprofileR()] (e.g. `nmf_rank`, `nmf_nrun`, `gprofiler_sources`, `nmf_parallel`, `enrichment_cache`). pass `expression_data`, `output_prefix`, `run_id`; driver sets per cohort. skip_existing Logical. `TRUE` (default), cohort whose results directory already exists re-run; summary loaded disk still included consolidated output. on_error One `"continue"` (default; log failing cohort carry ) `"stop"` (abort whole batch first cohort error). write_batch_summary Logical. `TRUE` (default) consolidated summary also written `output_dir/Batch_Consolidated_Summary.tsv`.

title: Run NMFprofileR over many cohorts and consolidate the results тАФ run_nmf_batch
text: Invisibly, list : `consolidated` data frame binding every cohort's       `consolidated_summary_df` (tagged `Run_ID`). `failures` data frame (`Cohort`, `Reason`) cohorts errored. `skipped` character vector cohorts skipped       results already existed.

title: Run NMFprofileR over many cohorts and consolidate the results тАФ run_nmf_batch
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data("example_expression_data") # Split the samples into two illustrative cohorts. half <- ncol(example_expression_data) %/% 2 cohorts <- list(   cohort_A = example_expression_data[, seq_len(half)],   cohort_B = example_expression_data[, (half + 1):ncol(example_expression_data)] ) batch <- run_nmf_batch(cohorts, output_dir = "batch_out", nmf_rank = 2:3, nmf_nrun = 10) batch$consolidated } # }

title: NMFprofileR 0.5.0
text: align_factors() matches NMF factors across runs (cohorts, ranks, resamples) basis-loading correlation reference run, returning full correlation table plus one--one matching (greedy default; hungarian via clue::solve_LSAP() clue package installed). nmf_project() scores new samples existing factorization solving coefficients basis matrix held fixed (non-negative multiplicative updates), returning per-sample factor scores assignments. nmf_stability() assesses factor reproducibility refitting subsamples samples reporting factorтАЩs mean best-match correlation full-data fit. NMFprofileR(stability_nboot = N) runs per rank (opt-, default), adding Stability column consolidated summary $stability element result. basis_gene_method selects genes assigned factors primary output: "argmax" (default, unchanged) "specificity" (Kim-Park markers).

title: NMFprofileR 0.4.0
text: run_nmf_batch() runs NMFprofileR() named list cohorts, writing subdirectory returning single consolidated per-factor summary tagged cohort Run_ID. resilient (on_error = "continue"), resumable (skip_existing reloads finished cohorts disk), writes Batch_Consolidated_Summary.tsv. Opt-parallel NMF: nmf_parallel = TRUE (optional nmf_cores) spreads consensus runs across local cluster. cluster loads NMF worker, works called inside package, fixed nmf_seed returns factorization sequential path. default remains sequential. g:Profiler hardening: oversized-query guard (max_query_size), automatic retry backoff transient network failures, optional -disk result cache (enrichment_cache) keyed query hash re-runs batches skip network.

title: NMFprofileR 0.4.0
text: New тАЬBatch analysis across many cohortsтАЭ vignette.

title: NMFprofileR 0.3.0
text: emit_marker_genes now defaults FALSE (previously TRUE). Specificity marker genes enrichment now opt-, halving g:Profiler network work default run. Set emit_marker_genes = TRUE restore previous behaviour. return value still always carries marker_genes enrichment$markers elements (empty markers ).

title: NMFprofileR 0.3.0
text: Themeable plots: NMFprofileR() gains custom_theme (ggplot2 theme applied ggplot-based plots) factor_palette (colour vector factors), figures can match projectтАЩs house style without editing package. umap_n_neighbors parametrizes sample-coefficient UMAP. embedding drawn whenever cohort samples value, neighbour count capped n_samples - 1, smaller cohorts now get UMAP . Self-describing outputs: optional run_id stamped leading Run_ID column consolidated summary recorded manifest; whole nmf_profile result saved single <prefix>_nmf_profile.rds bundle; Summaries/manifest.tsv lists every file run produced type. Failed ranks reported: result gains failures data frame (rank reason) print() lists failed ranks, instead ranks silently vanishing output.

title: NMFprofileR 0.2.0
text: pipeline now available composable, exported stages return results memory: nmf_preprocess(), nmf_fit(), nmf_basis_genes(), nmf_marker_genes(), nmf_sample_assignments(), nmf_enrichment(), nmf_rank_diagnostics(). NMFprofileR() orchestrates . NMFprofileR() returns S3 nmf_profile object (print() summary() methods) carrying fitted objects, gene assignments, enrichment tables memory, just file paths. write_files = FALSE runs pipeline entirely memory, creating files. -front input validation actionable errors, plus explicit duplicate gene-symbol handling via on_duplicate_genes (default "collapse_max"). Factor-specific marker genes (NMF::extractFeatures, Kim-Park specificity) emitted alongside argmax basis-gene assignment (emit_marker_genes, default TRUE). variance_scale selects raw- log-scale variance highly-variable-gene selection (default "raw", preserving previous behaviour). Reproducibility hardening: run records manifest, g:Profiler database version, session information; UMAP embedding seeded.

title: NMFprofileR 0.2.0
text: global expression heatmap now groups genes assignment used exported basis-gene table (previously figure table disagree). Cleared ggplot2 (>= 3.4) linewidth deprecation warnings. capture_plot() longer leaks graphics device plot expression fails. Clear error (instead opaque NMF crash) fewer two genes survive filtering.

title: NMFprofileR 0.2.0
text: Added testthat (edition 3) test suite, modernized R-CMD-check workflow (r-lib/actions matrix), added test-coverage workflow.

title: NMFprofileR 0.1.0
text: Initial version.
